title: Add PHP as a default language
author: shoulders
body: # Feature request I would like to like `PHP` to be added on to the default enabled languages as it is a great fit with the ones already selected. ## Summary Most amatuers start of with `HTML` and `CSS` for websites and then move on to `PHP`. Yes I am aware I can set my default languages etc.. but this is about adding `PHP` to the defaults. ## Examples thanks

title: Support Tokio documentation
author: 007qr
body:
# Feature request

## Summary

Add support for the Tokio documentation set to DevDocs.

Tokio is the de facto asynchronous runtime for Rust and is one of the most widely used libraries in the Rust ecosystem. Many Rust developers spend a significant amount of time referencing Tokio's documentation alongside the Rust standard library, making it a strong candidate for inclusion in DevDocs.

Having Tokio available offline and searchable through DevDocs would improve the experience for developers building async applications, web services, networking tools, and distributed systems in Rust.

## Examples

Official documentation:

* https://docs.rs/tokio/latest/tokio/

Repository:

* https://github.com/tokio-rs/tokio

Commonly referenced modules and APIs:

* `tokio::spawn`
* `tokio::task`
* `tokio::sync`
* `tokio::net`
* `tokio::time`
* `tokio::fs`
* `tokio::process`
* `tokio::select!`

Why this would be valuable:

* Tokio is the most popular async runtime in the Rust ecosystem.
* It is a dependency of many widely used Rust frameworks and libraries, including Axum, Hyper, and Tonic.
* Rust developers frequently need to search Tokio APIs while developing async applications.
* Offline access through DevDocs would make Rust development more productive and reduce context switching between multiple documentation sites.

author: simon04
body:
For the past few weeks, all deployments have failed since various files could not be downloaded from documents.devdocs.io/.../index.json due to Net::ReadTimeout

Oliver, would you be so kind to investigate this issue? Are we possibly rate-limiting our own deployment by a documents.devdocs.io configuration? Thank you!

Example: https://github.com/freeCodeCamp/devdocs/actions/runs/26403866257/job/77722443118

The table of contents should start with entry into the language or framework, then functional or class reference of it. 

This will make sure that everyone getting started on a language has a clear flow of knowledge from easy to expert, then for the experts, functional reference list will be a plus

## Examples
lets say we want to create a table of contents for html:

Table of Contents:  HTML
What is html ........................................................ 1
Evolution of HTML ............................................  2
some feature .......................................................  n

Windows 11 Pro ( and Home)

## Possible fix

Update the content scraper to detect CMake 4.x line.

title: Unit test frontend code
author: spamguy
body:
# Feature request

Cover frontend JavaScript with unit testing.

## Summary

While the Ruby backend core is fully covered by Minitest unit tests, the JavaScript frontend has no testing framework in place. This certainly encouraged past regressions and will continue to do so. It also omits a layer of expected behavior documentation.

Short of committing test framework packages to the repo, this would involve introducing a package.json file solely for dev dependencies. A dependency install step in dev setup instructions would need to be added.

(As an unrelated followup: with this in place, consider migrating vendor dependencies to this file.)

title: Broken Link Navigation in Nim and Nim 1
author: janAkali
body:
<!--
If possible fill each section

# Bug report

<!--
Verify this steps before writing a new issue:

 - Search for existing issues; it's possible someone has already encountered this bug.
-->
This affects both search results and links in the documentation.
When clicking most links in document - page scrolls to the top of the page, instead of the linked section.
When searching for symbol, if there's 2 or more symbols with same name - clicking one of them will work correctly, but the other scrolls to the top of the page.

Copying and pasting link manually works correctly.

## OS information

<!--
What operating system and browser version are you using?
-->

Linux 6.12 + Firefox 148
I can also replicate this on Mobile: Android 11 Firefox 147
(on mobile this only affects pressing links in the document, because UI reloads page when clicking search results)

<!--

encountered it
-->

1. go to https://devdocs.io
2. enable Nim
3. type `Nim<tab>log2` in search field
4. click on each `log2` symbol in the results

OR

1. go to https://devdocs.io/nim/math#log2%2Cfloat32
2. click on any link in "See also:" section

title: Proposal: Compressing DevDocs with a Low-Level C Core
author: CyberReaper00
body:
I’ve been using DevDocs for years—it’s a lifesaver when AI fails on niche cases. I have a lot of respect for the work done so far, but the current Ruby/JS stack feels like it’s holding the project back from being as fast and straight-forward as it could be

What i am asking is if I can rewrite the core server and processing logic in C? Specifically:

1. The Search Engine: Moving the indexing and fuzzy matching from JS to a C codebase that is compiled to a wasm module to kill the memory lag

2. The HTML Pipeline: Replacing the Ruby/Nokogiri filters with a fast C-based parser for cleaning docsets

3. The Local Server: Swapping the Ruby/Sinatra backend for a lightweight C static server to remove the heavy runtime requirements for local hosting

Due to such changes around 30% of the project may be reduced including the definite removal of something as useless as docker, which will be replaced with simple statically-compiled servers for each OS that people can just download (or build themselves since C is available on all platforms) and launch the server without any unnecessary dependencies

I'm happy to handle the heavy lifting and resolve any issues that pop up with this new setup. Are you guys open to adding this C-based paradigm to the official repo? I truly believe this would make the project much leaner and easier to maintain long-term

I truly hope that you will accept this crazy request to rebuild the core of the project in a language that most of your contributors probably dont use and I hope I get the chance to work with you on this

title: Alt + O keyboard shortcut not working - external documentation link fails to open
author: JithunMethusahan
body:
## Description
The keyboard shortcut `Alt + O` is not functioning. It should open the current documentation page in a new tab on the original documentation site, but nothing happens when the shortcut is pressed.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Visit https://devdocs.io
2. Enable any documentation (tested with "Git" and "Python")
3. Navigate to any documentation page
   - Example: https://devdocs.io/git/git-status
4. Press `Alt + O` (Windows/Linux) or `Option + O` (Mac)

## Expected Behavior
- A new browser tab should open
- The new tab should navigate to the original documentation source
- For example, on the git-status page, it should open: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-status

## Actual Behavior
- Nothing happens
- No new tab opens
- No error messages appear
- No console errors
- No network requests are made

## Environment
- **Browser:** Chrome 120.0.6099.130 (also tested in Firefox)
- **OS:** Windows 11
- **DevDocs:** Latest version (tested on https://devdocs.io)
- **Date:** March 9, 2026

## Testing Conducted

### Multiple Documentation Types
- ❌ Git documentation: Alt + O does nothing
- ❌ Python documentation: Alt + O does nothing
- **Conclusion:** Issue affects all documentation pages

### Browser Console
- Opened Developer Tools (F12)
- Monitored Console tab while pressing Alt + O
- **Result:** No JavaScript errors

### Network Activity
- Opened Developer Tools Network tab
- Pressed Alt + O
- **Result:** No network requests triggered

### Event Registration
- The keyboard shortcut appears to not be registered or is not triggering properly
- No event seems to fire when Alt + O is pressed

## Impact
- Users cannot quickly access original documentation sources
- Keyboard navigation is incomplete
- Documented feature is non-functional

## Proposed Solution
I would like to investigate and fix this issue. Potential areas to check:
1. Keyboard event handler registration
2. Alt + O key binding in the shortcuts system
3. External URL retrieval for current page
4. Browser compatibility issues

## I Can Help
I have web development experience and have contributed to open-source projects (recently improved documentation for melting-lang). I've already:
- Reproduced the bug thoroughly
- Tested across multiple scenarios
- Forked the repository
- Ready to investigate the codebase

Would appreciate guidance on:
- Which files handle keyboard shortcuts
- Where the Alt + O binding should be registered
- Any known issues with this feature

I'm ready to submit a PR once I identify the root cause.

## Additional Context
This appears to be a long-standing issue as the feature is documented but non-functional. The lack of console errors suggests the event handler may not be registered rather than failing during execution.

title: Persistent storage using navigator.storage.persist()
author: davidsm10
body:
There is the `navigator.storage.persist()` API that allows the storage for a site/origin to become persistent (and maybe increase the limit) and I think it would very useful to use it on this project to prevent the browser from deleting the docs if you are running low on storage or you haven't used this page in a while, or even to increase the storage limit for people that download many docs for offline use.
Of course it should be optional. I think the best way to add this would be adding a button in the offline data section that the user can choose to click if they want to make the storage persistent.

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/StorageManager/persist

title: Technology icons appear incorrect after a certain point
author: alistairgrom
body: # Bug report Technnology logos are incorrect after a few in the list. They seem to be fine from A-C then some point from C there is an issue which causes an off by one mis-match and the logos do not match up correctly. ## OS information Macos 15.6.1 (24G90) Chrome Version 144.0.7559.112 (Official Build) (arm64) ## Steps to reproduce Opening site onto https://devdocs.io/ ## More resources Appears correct for the first few: Then by around C, they are off by one: ## Possible fix Icon directory mismatch possibly, in the `#icon-dir` of this file `assets/javascripts/templates/path_tmpl.js`
